title: pavonia glutinosa
intro: Pavonia glutinosa, also known as the sticky rose mallow, is a perennial plant species in the mallow family that grows in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. The medicinal properties of Pavonia glutinosa have been linked to various health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved wound healing, as well as potential applications in treating conditions such as skin infections and gastrointestinal disorders. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Pavonia glutinosa can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to soothe skin irritations and promote healing.
